page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores using the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a ratio of 8 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is provided locally via 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r6idn.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 61937.6 and a Passmark CPU score of 57772.18. The processor's support for advanced vector extensions (AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI) contributes to strong performance in specialized workloads, including a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 53865.21 and a ray tracing score of 54867.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 3317.67 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds reach 167.06 GB/sec and 67.91 GB/sec, respectively.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications that require high-speed local storage and substantial network throughput. Strong results in Passmark database operations (26521.81) and static web serving throughput (45.56 GB/sec) indicate suitability for large-scale databases, caching layers, and high-traffic web applications. Additionally, the instance achieves 27.91 tokens/sec in text generation for a medium 7B LLM model, making it viable for CPU-based inference tasks. The combination of high memory density, local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking provides a balanced profile for enterprise workloads without the need for separate storage provisioning.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61937.6 and a Passmark CPU score of 57772.18. The hardware configuration is highly effective for data-intensive tasks, showing strong results in database operations and static web serving. This instance is well-suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, real-time analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 64 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
512 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory (8 GB per core ratio)
3800 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
